title: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme
content: The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization. Eligibility: Must be recognized by DPIIT, incorporated less than 2 years ago, and must not have received more than 10 Lakh rupees from other government schemes. Terms: Up to 20 Lakhs as grant for validation, or up to 50 Lakhs as convertible debentures for market entry.

title: First 100 Users Strategy
content: To get your first 100 users, do not use paid ads. Instead: 1) Identify where your niche hangs out (Reddit, Discord, specialized forums). 2) Do unscalable direct outreach via LinkedIn or Twitter DMs. 3) Leverage your personal network. 4) Launch on directories like Product Hunt or BetaList. Your goal is extreme feedback, not revenue.

title: Y Combinator Standard Deal
content: Y Combinator (YC) invests $500,000 in every startup accepted into their batch. The standard deal is $125,000 on a post-money SAFE for 7% of the company, plus a $375,000 uncapped SAFE with a Most Favored Nation (MFN) clause.
